definitions.text: A point; the sharp end or top of anything that terminates in a point; as, the peak, or front, of a cap.
The highest value reached by some quantity in a time period.
examples: The stock market reached a peak in September 1929.

definitions.text: The top, or one of the tops, of a hill, mountain, or range, ending in a point.
examples: They reached the peak after 8 hours of climbing.

definitions.text: To raise the point of (a gaff) closer to perpendicular.
To exceed the maximum signal amplitude of (a piece of equipment), resulting in clipping of the signal.
To reach a highest degree or maximum.
examples: Historians argue about when the Roman Empire began to peak and ultimately decay.

definitions.text: Maximal, quintessential, archetypical, strictly with positive connotations; representing the culmination of its type.
examples: Knowing obscure 19th-century slang is peak nerd.

definitions.text: To acquire sharpness of figure or features; hence, to look thin or sickly.
